edges.description: Loss of wall integrity can progress from dilatation to rupture with hematoma, gastrointestinal bleeding, or shock.
Increased flow and wall stress can drive remodeling of the splenic arterial wall.
Local geometry-driven stress can promote focal dilatation.
Pregnancy-related hemodynamic and hormonal stress increases concern for rupture.

edges.source: Open surgical repair, ligation, resection, or splenectomy
Open surgical repair, ligation, resection, or splenectomy
Selective surveillance of small stable intact true aneurysms

nodes.description: Rupture of a splenic artery aneurysm causes internal bleeding that may present with abdominal pain, hematoma, gastrointestinal bleeding, hypotension, syncope, or hypovolemic shock.

evidence.reference_title: Spontaneous Rupture of Splenic Artery Aneurysm.
evidence.snippet: The patient presented in the emergency department with abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting, followed by syncope.

evidence.reference_title: Giant splenic artery aneurysm rupture into the stomach that was successfully managed with emergency distal pancreatectomy.
evidence.snippet: He developed upper gastrointestinal (UGI) bleeding and hypovolemic shock.

nodes.description: Coil embolization excludes the aneurysm or parent artery from flow and is a common first-line approach for intact anatomically suitable SAA.

evidence.reference_title: Endovascular and Surgical Management of Intact Splenic Artery Aneurysm.
evidence.snippet: In the endovascular group, the exclusive means was embolization with coils.

evidence.reference_title: Endovascular and Surgical Management of Intact Splenic Artery Aneurysm.
evidence.snippet: Endovascular repair is less invasive accompanied with an obvious decrease in surgery time and rapid recovery with a short hospital time.

nodes.description: Open repair includes aneurysm ligation or resection, sometimes with splenectomy, arterial reconstruction, or distal pancreatic resection, especially for rupture, giant lesions, unfavorable anatomy, or instability.
nodes.id: Open surgical repair, ligation, resection, or splenectomy

evidence.reference_title: Endovascular and Surgical Management of Intact Splenic Artery Aneurysm.
evidence.snippet: In the surgical group, the common methods used were splenic artery aneurysm resection (9 patients), followed by splenic artery aneurysms resection and splenectomy (6 patients), splenic artery aneurysm resection and arterial reconstruction with end-to-end anastomosis (3 patients), and laparoscopic splenic artery aneurysm resection coexisting with splenectomy (2 patients).

evidence.reference_title: Spontaneous Rupture of Splenic Artery Aneurysm.
evidence.snippet: The patient was submitted to emergency laparotomy with ligation of the splenic artery, aneurysm resection, and splenectomy.

nodes.description: Portal hypertension can increase splenic arterial diameter and flow, creating a hyperkinetic splenic circulation that increases mechanical stress on the splenic artery and favors aneurysm formation or intervention.

evidence.reference_title: Splenic hyperkinetic state and splenic artery aneurysm in portal hypertension.
evidence.snippet: In the portal hypertensive patients with splenic artery aneurysms, the splenic artery was larger (p < 0.05) and the splenic arterial flow greater (p < 0.05), and these patients were in a more hyperkinetic state, than were those with no splenic artery aneurysm.

nodes.description: Pregnancy is a high-risk physiologic context for SAA because gestational hormonal influences and portal-flow changes can interact with pre-existing wall vulnerability, and rupture has very high maternal and fetal mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Splenic aneurysms: natural history and treatment techniques.
evidence.snippet: Among pregnant patients, mortality after rupture is 65-75%, with fetal mortality exceeding 90%.

evidence.reference_title: Splenic aneurysms: natural history and treatment techniques.
evidence.snippet: There are multiple etiologies and it is believed that hormonal influences and changes in portal flow during gestation play an important role in development of SAA.

nodes.description: Selected intact asymptomatic true SAAs can be monitored rather than repaired immediately when high-risk features such as childbearing potential, symptoms, growth, large size, or pseudoaneurysm are absent.
nodes.id: Selective surveillance of small stable intact true aneurysms

evidence.reference_title: Splenic artery aneurysms: two decades experience at Mayo clinic.
evidence.snippet: From analysis of the patient data we concluded that although SAAs may rupture, not all intact aneurysms need intervention.

evidence.reference_title: The Society for Vascular Surgery clinical practice guidelines on the management of visceral aneurysms.
evidence.snippet: They include evidence-based size thresholds for repair of aneurysms of the renal arteries, splenic artery, celiac artery, and hepatic artery, among others.

nodes.description: Multifactorial splenic arterial wall remodeling weakens the vessel wall and permits focal aneurysmal dilatation. The structured evidence base for isolated true SAA is mostly clinical, so this node represents the shared vessel-wall failure endpoint rather than a single molecular cause.

evidence.reference_title: Splenic aneurysms: natural history and treatment techniques.
evidence.snippet: There are multiple etiologies and it is believed that hormonal influences and changes in portal flow during gestation play an important role in development of SAA.

nodes.description: The defining finding is aneurysmal dilatation of the splenic artery, detected by vascular imaging, angiography, operation, or pathology.

evidence.reference_title: Splenic aneurysms: natural history and treatment techniques.
evidence.snippet: True splenic artery aneurysms (SAA) are a rare, but potentially fatal, pathology.

nodes.description: Tortuous splenic artery geometry may concentrate hemodynamic forces and correlate with aneurysm formation and dilatation, especially in female patients.

evidence.reference_title: Morphological analysis using geometric parameters for splenic aneurysms.
evidence.snippet: Background Considering the unique characteristics of splenic artery aneurysms, we hypothesized that hemodynamic forces could play an important role in splenic artery aneurysm formation and that splenic artery geometry should be correlated with aneurysm development.

evidence.reference_title: Morphological analysis using geometric parameters for splenic aneurysms.
evidence.snippet: Conclusion Females with a tortuous splenic artery may have an increased risk of aneurysm formation.
